problem: How many ways are there to divide a group of 6 friends among the basketball team, the soccer team, and the track team?  (Each team could have anywhere from 0 to 6 of the friends on it.  Assume the friends are distinguishable.)

classified_questions.question: What is the initial step in determining the number of ways to divide a group of friends among three teams?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What does it mean for the friends to be distinguishable in this problem?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why there are three options for each friend to be placed on a team?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the significance of raising three to the sixth power in determining the total number of ways the friends can be divided among the three teams?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Is it possible to divide the group of friends in such a way that one team has all six friends on it? If so, how many ways are there to do this?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are some other scenarios in which the principle of counting and probability could be applied in similar ways?
classified_questions.label: r
